installation.scope: The current public SDK version is a prerelease. pip excludes prereleases unless --pre or an exact prerelease version is requested.

proof.scope: ViewSpec prove validates intent, compiles through the public local path, checks the artifact manifest and hashes, records compact style-delta counts when profiles are present, writes PROOF.md for humans, proof_report.json for tools, redacted support_bundle.json for local support triage, and can optionally run the bounded React Tailwind reference host proof.
proof.non_claim: ViewSpec prove is not pixel-perfect visual regression, accessibility certification, arbitrary host-app certification, or hosted compiler publish automation.

freerange.prerequisite: A user-installed stable Bun 1.x executable on PATH; ViewSpec never installs Bun.
freerange.scope: Freerange analyzes only the manifest-described generated numeric kernel and its recorded call-site hashes.
freerange.non_claim: The Freerange integration does not analyze CSS, prove rendered geometry, certify arbitrary math, or certify arbitrary host applications.

pretext.prerequisite: The generated React app npm/Chromium proof toolchain; Pretext does not require Bun.
pretext.scope: Pretext preserves native DOM semantics and compares only native-DOM and Pretext line counts and overflow under the named Arial profile at the three canonical viewports.
pretext.non_claim: The Pretext integration is not general typography proof, canvas-rendering proof, pixel-perfect visual regression, accessibility certification, or arbitrary-host certification.

composed.scope: The combined command composes the independently reported Freerange numeric-kernel and Pretext native-DOM line-count/overflow analyses.
composed.non_claim: Composition does not broaden either integration's bounded guarantees.

e2e.scope: Dedicated hosted E2E is configured for standalone Pretext and composed Freerange plus Pretext proofs; this status does not claim a hosted run has completed.

core_refinement.scope: A fixed ten-case corpus of supported briefs passed the product-quality gate and rendered conformantly at canonical mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports with accessibility, DOM, log, and screenshot evidence. Every case also completed one bounded semantic correction with a verified preview and applied receipt.
core_refinement.non_claim: This fixed regression corpus is evidence for the supported brief families and checked revision; it is not a guarantee that every arbitrary product brief is desirable, accessible, or production-ready.

appbundle_state_ir.scope: AppBundle V3 is a bounded local app contract for static routes, embedded screen IntentBundles, fixture_readonly_v0 resource views, interactive_state_v0 state, generated reducer artifacts, state_replay_assertions, and html-tailwind-app shell proof. Schema_version 4 adds bounded visibility_v0 rules with baked initial visibility and a replay-proved evaluateViewSpecVisibility reducer export.
appbundle_state_ir.non_claim: State IR is not Redux, Zustand, CRDT, persistence, backend generation, browser-history proof, live data or text rebinding, or a deployable framework app.

aesthetic_profiles.scope: Aesthetic Profiles V1 are deterministic view-level art-direction handles. The homepage switches between eight compiled landing artifacts while preserving the same semantic graph, stable semantic ids, zero shell overrides, governed style projections, bounded layout metadata, compact style-delta counts in checked manifest summaries, and closed React Tailwind recipes.
aesthetic_profiles.non_claim: Aesthetic profiles are not arbitrary CSS, pixel-perfect visual proof, accessibility certification, arbitrary host-app compatibility, or design-review approval.

accessibility.contrast_standard: WCAG 2.x AA, size/role-scoped: body text 4.5:1, large text and UI components 3.0:1
accessibility.scope: ViewSpec prove enumerates every governed text/background pair from the closed style vocabulary plus the emitter's fixed base pairs (and, for the react-tailwind-tsx emitter, the closed Tailwind recipes resolved against the browser-grounded Tailwind v4 palette), fails closed when any pair is below its scoped WCAG AA threshold, and fails closed when an interactive control (input, button, image) lacks an author-provided accessible name (a generic fallback such as the binding id does not count; a form input associated with its visible field label counts as named).
accessibility.non_claim: Scoped contrast (html-tailwind and react-tailwind-tsx) and accessible-name presence are checked, not a full WCAG audit, screen-reader certification, or focus-order or keyboard-operability proof.
